turns.content: I'm trying to build a custom Layer in tracing-subscriber but I keep thinking the Filter trait is enough on its own. Can't I just implement Filter and pass it down?

Filter alone won't work for your use case because it only handles events, not fields or spans. You need a Layer that implements the Subscriber trait to intercept calls before they reach the writer. The Filter trait is just one component of the full tracing-subscriber architecture.
Okay so I need to implement Subscriber? But how do I handle field filtering specifically without writing a whole new library?

You can reuse an existing crate like tracing-appender or write a minimal wrapper that implements Subscriber. The key is implementing the record method to check your filter logic before calling down to the writer. This way you keep the filtering logic encapsulated and avoid duplicating code.
That makes sense, but I'm worried about performance since every event has to pass through my custom layer. Is there a way to skip certain events entirely?

Yes, you can use the filter field in your Subscriber implementation to decide whether to record or discard an event early. If your filter returns false, the event never reaches the writer, which avoids unnecessary processing overhead.
I see, so I'm building a Layer that filters by target and writes to custom output? But how do I test this without breaking my existing setup?

You can use tracing-test or write a simple unit test that creates a Subscriber instance with your filter logic. This lets you verify the filtering behavior in isolation before integrating it into your full trace configuration.
Wait, so I don't need to rebuild my entire tracing-subscriber setup? Just swap in this Layer?
Exactly. You can build a Layer that implements Subscriber and plug it into your subscriber chain without touching the rest of your configuration.
